title: Consistent Overachievers
description: You are given three tables: employees, sales targets per quarter, and actual sales per quarter. Find names of employees who beat their target (revenue > goal) in at least 2 quarters, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
employees(id, name, dept)
targets(id, employee_id, quarter, goal)
sales(id, employee_id, quarter, revenue)
outputFormat: One employee name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use two JOINs. Join targets and sales on both employee_id AND quarter. Filter WHERE revenue > goal. Use HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN targets t ON e.id = t.employee_id JOIN sales s ON e.id = s.employee_id AND t.quarter = s.quarter WHERE s.revenue > t.goal GROUP BY e.id, e.name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY e.name;
hint: Join employees → targets → sales on BOTH employee_id AND quarter, filter revenue > goal, HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept TEXT); CREATE TABLE targets (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, quarter TEXT, goal INTEGER); CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, quarter TEXT, revenue INTEGER); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Alice','Sales'),(2,'Bob','Sales'),(3,'Carol','Sales'); INSERT INTO targets VALUES (1,1,'Q1',10000),(2,1,'Q2',12000),(3,2,'Q1',8000),(4,2,'Q2',9000),(5,3,'Q1',15000); INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1,1,'Q1',11000),(2,1,'Q2',13000),(3,2,'Q1',9000),(4,2,'Q2',8000),(5,3,'Q1',14000);
